prompt: Using ProteinMPNN, design solubilised recombinant variants of West Nile virus envelope protein E ectodomain (PDB 2HG0) with improved expression yield in Drosophila S2 insect cells for use as a diagnostic antigen and vaccine candidate. Apply entropy-reduction and hydrophilicity constraints to surface residues. outline a computational approach covering: (1) ProteinMPNN sampling with dimer interface partially masked for monomer production, (2) positive design for neutralising-epitope loop geometry preservation (domain III), (3) CamSol solubility scoring and ranking, (4) validation by expression trial, SEC-MALS, and anti-WNV antibody ELISA. Discuss cross-reactivity considerations with other flavivirus antigens (dengue, Zika) in serodiagnostics.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and RCSB PDB (2HG0); target: Envelope protein E ectodomain; organism: West Nile virus lineage 1; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: flavivirus surface glycoprotein (dimeric class II membrane fusion protein)
